with open('/mnt/data/text_to_analyse.txt','r',encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
text[:1000]
import os, json, textwrap, pprint, re, pathlib, glob, sys, itertools
list(pathlib.Path('/mnt/data').glob('**/*'))[:20]

with open('/mnt/data/file-EvEoo892HqR3zrTXxbACG4-Q042.txt','r',encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
print(text[:1000])
print(text[1000:2000])
print(text[2000:3000])
import re, pprint, textwrap, itertools, json, collections

# find segments within braces {}
pattern = re.compile(r'\{([^{}]+)\}')
segments = [(m.group(0), m.start(), m.end()) for m in pattern.finditer(text)]
len(segments), segments[:3]
segments[3]

verse2_text = "وَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ"
text.find(verse2_text)
print(text[720:820])

# find next newline after that
start = text.find(verse2_text)
end = text.find('\n', start)
text[start:end]
print(text[930:1200])

hadith_start = text.find('«')
hadith_start

hadith_end = text.find('\n', hadith_start)
text[hadith_start:hadith_end]

spans = []

# pattern to get braces segments with start-end inside braces
for m in pattern.finditer(text):
    span_start = m.start() + 1  # exclude opening {
    span_end = m.end() - 1      # exclude closing }
    span_text = text[span_start: span_end]
    spans.append(('Aya', span_start, span_end, span_text))

# verse outside braces:
verse2_start = text.find(verse2_text)
verse2_end = text.find('\n', verse2_start)
if verse2_end == -1:
    verse2_end = len(text)
span2_text = text[verse2_start: verse2_end].rstrip()
spans.append(('Aya', verse2_start, verse2_end-len(text[verse2_end:]), span2_text))  # wait formula?

spans = []
for m in pattern.finditer(text):
    span_start = m.start() + 1
    span_end = m.end() - 1
    span_text = text[span_start: span_end]
    spans.append({'type':'Aya', 'start': span_start, 'end': span_end, 'text': span_text})
# external verse:
verse2_start = text.find(verse2_text)
assert verse2_start != -1
verse2_end_linebreak = text.find('\n', verse2_start)
if verse2_end_linebreak == -1:
    verse2_end_linebreak = len(text)
verse2_span_text = text[verse2_start:verse2_end_linebreak]
spans.append({'type':'Aya', 'start': verse2_start, 'end': verse2_end_linebreak, 'text': verse2_span_text})

# hadith
hadith_start = text.find('«')
if hadith_start != -1:
    hadith_end = text.find('\n', hadith_start)
    if hadith_end == -1:
        hadith_end = len(text)
    hadith_span_text = text[hadith_start+1:hadith_end]  # exclude «
    # also need to exclude closing » if exists
    if hadith_span_text.endswith('»'):
        hadith_span_text = hadith_span_text[:-1]
        # adjust end accordingly
        hadith_end_adj = hadith_end - 1
    else:
        hadith_end_adj = hadith_end
    spans.append({'type':'Hadeeth', 'start': hadith_start + 1, 'end': hadith_end_adj, 'text': hadith_span_text})
len(spans)
spans

output.code:
citations = []
for span in spans:
    entry = {
        "span_start": span['start'],
        "span_end": span['end'],
        "span_text": span['text'],
        "citation_type": "Aya" if span['type']=='Aya' else "Hadeeth",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {} if span['type']=='Aya' else {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {} if span['type']=='Hadeeth' else {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    }
    citations.append(entry)
citations

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
